target.title: fix(exec): auto-approve recognized read-only boolean flags on default safe bins
target.body:
## Summary

Default safe bins (`cut`, `head`, `tail`, `tr`, `uniq`, `wc`) exist to auto-approve **stdin-only text filters** without a manual approval prompt. But the short-option validator only had an *accept* path for **value-consuming** flags: `consumeShortOptionClusterToken` skipped any flag not in `allowedValueFlags` and, if the cluster contained no value flag, fell through to a terminal `return -1` (reject). So a cluster of pure **boolean** short flags was never auto-approved.

Net effect: the most common read-only invocations of these bins — `wc -l`, `wc -w`, `tr -d`, `tr -s`, `uniq -c` (and `sort -n`/`-r` for operators who add `sort`) — were force-routed to manual approval, even though the bins are stdin-only and every dangerous flag is already in `deniedFlags`. The presence of short flags in `deniedFlags` (`sort: -o, -T`; `jq: -L, -f`; `grep: -f, -r, -R`) shows the intended model is a denylist over recognized flags, so rejecting legitimate boolean flags is an oversight, not a deliberate narrowing.

## Fix

Add an `allowedBooleanFlags` allowlist to the safe-bin profile model and populate it for the six default bins with their read-only, no-side-effect boolean flags:

- `wc`: `-l -w -c -m -L` (+ long forms)
- `tr`: `-d -s -c -C -t`
- `uniq`: `-c -d -u -i -z`
- `cut`: `-s -n -z --complement --only-delimited`
- `head` / `tail`: `-q -v -z` (+ `--quiet/--silent/--verbose/--zero-terminated`). `tail -f`/`-F`/`--follow`/`--retry` are deliberately **excluded** so the auto-approve path can never stream/hang.

The validator now treats a recognized boolean flag as "no value, keep scanning" and accepts a cluster made entirely of recognized boolean flags, while an **unrecognized** short flag still hits `return -1` (fail-closed). The long-option path needs no logic change: feeding boolean long flags into `knownLongFlags` makes `consumeLongOptionToken`'s existing `if (!expectsValue) return index + 1` branch reachable for the first time. Denied flags are still checked first, so `sort -o`, `wc --files0-from`, etc. remain rejected.

This is intentionally **not** exposed as a new `tools.exec.safeBinProfiles` config field (root `AGENTS.md`: the config/env surface bar is high). It fixes the curated built-in default-bin profiles only; custom user profiles keep the existing `allowedValueFlags` / `deniedFlags` model. `sort`/`grep`/`jq` are not default safe bins and are left for a follow-up.

## Behavior change

| Invocation | before | after |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `wc -l`, `wc -w`, `wc --lines` | needs approval | **auto-approved** |
| `tr -d abc`, `tr -s ' '` | needs approval | **auto-approved** |
| `uniq -c`, `cut -s -f 1` | needs approval | **auto-approved** |
| `tr -S a b` (unknown short flag) | needs approval | needs approval (unchanged) |
| `sort -o out.txt` (denied) | needs approval | needs approval (unchanged) |
| `wc --files0-from=list` (denied) | needs approval | needs approval (unchanged) |

## Diff size

```
 docs/tools/exec-approvals-advanced.md       |  4 +-
 src/infra/exec-approvals-safe-bins.test.ts  | 32 +++++++++++++
 src/infra/exec-safe-bin-policy-profiles.ts  | 73 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 src/infra/exec-safe-bin-policy-validator.ts | 23 +++++----
 src/infra/exec-safe-bin-policy.test.ts      | 30 ++++++++++++
 5 files changed, 152 insertions(+), 10 deletions(-)
```

## Test plan

- [x] New validator unit tests (`exec-safe-bin-policy.test.ts`): boolean short flags accepted (`wc -l`, `-lw`, `uniq -c`, `tr -d`), boolean long flags + abbreviations (`wc --lines`, `--word`), value-on-boolean rejected (`wc --lines=5`), unknown short flags rejected (`tr -S`, `wc -Z`), mixed boolean+value cluster preserved (`uniq -cf 2`).
- [x] New end-to-end-gate tests (`exec-approvals-safe-bins.test.ts`) through `isSafeBinUsage`: `wc -l`, `wc --words`, `uniq -c`, `tr -d abc` auto-allowed; the existing `tr -S a b` -> rejected guard kept.
- [x] Full `exec-safe-bin-policy.test.ts` + `exec-approvals-safe-bins.test.ts`: 155 passed (incl. denied-flag matrix and docs-parity).
- [x] `tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json` clean for the changed files; `oxlint` clean on changed files.
- [ ] CI + clawsweeper re-review.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** default safe bins are documented as auto-approved stdin-only text filters, but the safe-bin argv validator rejected pure boolean short-flag clusters, so `wc -l`, `tr -d`, `uniq -c`, `sort -n` were routed to manual approval. After the patch the real auto-approve gate (`isSafeBinUsage`) returns `true` for those recognized read-only boolean forms, while unknown/denied flags stay fail-closed.

**Real environment tested:** Linux 6.8.0-71-generic, Node v22.19, real repo source at branch head `34cef99a` vs `origin/main` (`ec6ad888`), driving the **real exported** `isSafeBinUsage` / `normalizeSafeBins` from `src/infra/exec-approvals.ts` via `tsx` — not a stub or a re-implementation. Resolutions use `/usr/bin/<bin>` (a default trusted dir) so the full path/trust/profile gate runs.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ran `tsx` over a harness that imports the real `isSafeBinUsage` and calls it for each argv, first on this branch (after) then with the two prod files reset to `origin/main` (before):

```
node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/safebin-proof.ts          # after = branch head
git checkout origin/main -- <profiles> <validator>   # revert prod files only
node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/safebin-proof.ts          # before = origin/main
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```
############ BEFORE (origin/main) ############
. wc -l                    -> needs approval
. wc -w                    -> needs approval
. wc --lines               -> needs approval
. tr -d abc                -> needs approval
. tr -s                    -> needs approval
. uniq -c                  -> needs approval
. cut -s -f 1              -> needs approval
. tr -S a b                -> needs approval
. sort -o out.txt          -> needs approval
. wc --files0-from=list    -> needs approval

############ AFTER (this branch) ############
> wc -l                    -> AUTO-APPROVED
> wc -w                    -> AUTO-APPROVED
> wc --lines               -> AUTO-APPROVED
> tr -d abc                -> AUTO-APPROVED
> tr -s                    -> AUTO-APPROVED
> uniq -c                  -> AUTO-APPROVED
> cut -s -f 1              -> AUTO-APPROVED
. tr -S a b                -> needs approval
. sort -o out.txt          -> needs approval
. wc --files0-from=list    -> needs approval
```

**Observed result after fix:** every recognized read-only boolean form flipped from *needs approval* to *AUTO-APPROVED*; the unknown short flag (`tr -S`) and the denied flags (`sort -o`, `wc --files0-from`) stayed fail-closed. Matches the new unit/gate tests.

**What was not tested:** a full gateway + live agent exec round-trip (model issues `wc -l` -> no approval prompt) — proof was taken at the real `isSafeBinUsage` decision boundary, which is the single gate that turns a safe-bin invocation into an auto-approval. No Windows path (safe bins are disabled on Windows by design).

target.title: fix(tui): preserve Python underscore identifiers
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve underscore identifiers (`__init__`, `is_palindrome`) in TUI Markdown rendering by escaping underscores before pi-tui/marked can treat them as emphasis.
- Generic single-regex approach: one `UNDERSCORE_ID_RE` catches both `snake_case` and `__dunder__` patterns, no language-specific dictionary or context heuristics needed.
- Protected regions (fenced code, inline code, HTML tags, links, URLs) are excluded from escaping so existing Markdown features continue to work.

**Behavior tradeoff:** `__word__` Markdown strong emphasis is no longer rendered in chat rows. `**bold**` emphasis still works and is the preferred form. This is acceptable because the TUI renders code-heavy content where identifier preservation matters more than `__...__` emphasis.

Fixes #90769.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/tui/components/hyperlink-markdown.ts src/tui/components/chat-log.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/tui/components/chat-log.test.ts` — 24/24 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts` — 14/14 passed
- `git diff --check` — clean
- Rebased onto latest `origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: TUI Markdown rendering now preserves Python dunder identifiers (`__name__`, `__init__`) and snake_case identifiers (`is_palindrome`) without consuming underscores as emphasis markup.
Real environment tested: local macOS source checkout with the OpenClaw fake-backend TUI PTY harness running the real `runTui()` terminal loop.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts`.
Evidence after fix: PTY harness test `renders Python dunder identifiers through the terminal markdown path` waits for visible terminal output containing `Use __name__ == "__main__" before calling __init__().`, `if __name__ == "__main__":`, `def is_palindrome(s: str) -> bool:`, `__name__`, `is_palindrome`, and `stay literal.`.
Observed result after fix: the PTY lane passed with 14 tests, and the ChatLog component lane passed with 24 tests covering fenced code, inline code, dunder prose, long fences, raw HTML tags, and identifier escaping.
What was not tested: live provider calls were not run locally because they require live provider credentials.

target.title: Fix LM Studio wizard prompter binding
target.body:
## Summary
- Bind LM Studio setup prompter methods before storing them as callbacks so gateway wizard class methods keep their receiver.
- Add regression coverage for both `text` and `note` class-method prompter paths.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Windows setup wizard selecting **More… → LM Studio** crashed on gateway `2026.5.28` with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'prompt')`.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows `openclaw-windows-node` setup UI against a local `OpenClawGateway` WSL gateway. The installed gateway bundle was patched with this PR's LM Studio prompter binding change for B-side validation.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Restarted `openclaw-gateway.service`, launched the Windows setup wizard, selected **More… → LM Studio**, and reran the headless LM Studio provider probe.
- **Evidence after fix:** Screen recording: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/30542ff5-59de-4e1d-b5d2-16c37cd985ef
- **Observed result after fix:** The wizard advanced to the `LM Studio base URL` step instead of throwing the prompt TypeError.
- **What was not tested:** Windows app consumption of an official fixed gateway release/LKG; that remains a follow-up after this upstream fix lands and a fixed gateway version is selected.

## Verification
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm test extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.test.ts` — 38 passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.ts extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.test.ts`
- `pnpm lint:extensions`
- Hanselman dual-model review: no blocking findings. Both reviewers flagged missing `note` binding coverage; this PR now includes that regression test.

## Notes
- `pnpm check:changed` could not run locally because the Crabbox/Testbox wrapper failed its local binary sanity check before starting the remote check.
- Windows consumption should wait for a fixed gateway release/LKG decision; this PR only fixes the gateway-side provider bug.

target.title: Create cron-main-channel-targeting.md
target.body:
Summary
What problem does this PR solve?
Cron jobs using sessionTarget: main have no way to specify a target delivery channel. For agents bound to multiple Slack channels, the output channel is non-deterministic — it depends on which channel last had activity in the main session. This makes reliable, channel-specific cron delivery impossible without switching to isolated mode.
Why does this matter now?
Multi-channel agents are a common pattern (e.g. a single agent serving several account channels). Without channel targeting in main mode, operators must choose between reply continuity (main) and delivery guarantees (isolated) — there is no option that provides both.
What is the intended outcome?
Support a to: field (or equivalent channel-targeting parameter) in sessionTarget: main cron job configuration, so the output is always delivered to the specified channel regardless of session history.
What is intentionally out of scope?
Changes to isolated mode behavior; changes to how replies are routed after delivery.
What does success look like?
A cron job configured with sessionTarget: main and a to: <channel> field consistently delivers its output to the specified channel, even when the agent is active in other channels.
What should reviewers focus on?
Whether the proposed to: parameter is the right API surface, or whether an alternative (e.g. delivery.channel unified across both session targets) is preferred.
───
Linked context
Which issue does this close?
Closes # (open a matching issue first if one doesn't exist, then link it here)
Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?
Related # (link any Discord discussion or forum thread)
Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?
No — this is a community-raised limitation discovered in production use of multi-channel agents.
───
Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
• Behavior or issue addressed: Cron job with sessionTarget: main delivering to the wrong Slack channel when agent is active in multiple channels
• Real environment tested: Self-hosted OpenClaw on AWS EC2, multiple Slack channels bound to a single agent
• Exact steps or command run after this patch: N/A — this is a feature proposal PR, no implementation patch included
• Evidence after fix: N/A
• Observed result after fix: N/A
• What was not tested: Full implementation — this PR proposes the feature; implementation and testing would follow maintainer feedback
• Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is a design/feature proposal. Real behavior proof will be added once an implementation approach is agreed upon.
───
Tests and validation
Which commands did you run? N/A — proposal only
What regression coverage was added or updated? None at this stage
What failed before this fix, if known? Cron output delivered to wrong channel in multi-channel agent setup
If no test was added, why not? No implementation yet — this PR is to propose and discuss the feature before writing code
───
Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? No (proposal only)
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No
What is the highest-risk area? Session routing logic for main mode cron jobs
How is that risk mitigated? To be determined by maintainers during design review
───
Current review state
What is the next action? Maintainer feedback on whether to: in main mode is the right approach, or if an alternative API is preferred
What is still waiting on? Maintainer input before any implementation begins
Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? None yet — initial submission

target.title: feat(talk): add macOS realtime relay mode
target.body:
## Summary

- add a provider-neutral macOS Talk realtime relay path backed by `talk.session.*` Gateway RPCs
- require explicit `talk.realtime.mode = realtime` plus `talk.realtime.transport = gateway-relay` before native macOS Talk enters the realtime relay path
- keep the existing native STT/TTS fallback when realtime relay config is missing or uses another transport
- deliver realtime agent-consult final text back to the active realtime Talk provider through a Talk-local final speech path
- require realtime providers to implement `speakText` for final speech; do not emulate final speech by sending provider-visible user messages
- parse structured Talk finals so the macOS voice layer speaks only the `response`/`spokenText` field
- preserve typed relay tool payload/result values at the `AnyCodable` boundary
- keep generic `chat.send` request/schema semantics unchanged

## Scope Reduction

- removed the generic chat-run final observer and adapter; Talk no longer hooks `chat.send`, chat final/error broadcasts, Gateway request context, or runtime chat subscriptions
- removed Android changes from this PR; Android remains on the existing path
- removed the optional HUD HTTP mirror and config schema; clients can consume the existing `talk.event` relay state without this PR adding local HTTP side effects
- removed the now-unused chat-run-final relay export and tests

## Validation

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `/private/tmp/openclaw-pnpm-bin/pnpm tsgo:core`
- `/private/tmp/openclaw-pnpm-bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/talk-realtime-relay.test.ts src/talk/consult-question.test.ts src/talk/session-runtime.test.ts`: 3 files, 51 tests passed
- `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter TalkResponseEnvelopeTests`: 5 tests passed
- `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter TalkPromptBuilderTests`: 4 tests passed
- `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter TalkModeGatewayConfigTests`: 4 tests passed

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Native macOS Talk should only enter realtime relay when explicitly configured for `gateway-relay`; OpenClaw final text should be delivered through the active realtime Talk provider as final speech; final speech delivery should not add a new `chat.send` request field or route provider TTS through a fake user message.

- **Real environment tested:** macOS live OpenClaw setup on this workstation, with the running signed GUI app at `/Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app/Contents/MacOS/OpenClaw` and the local Gateway reachable at `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`. The running app is Developer ID signed by `Developer ID Application: Peter Steinberger (Y5PE65HELJ)`, has Team ID `Y5PE65HELJ`, has a stapled notarization ticket, and passes Gatekeeper assessment as `Notarized Developer ID`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
$ git rev-parse --short=12 HEAD
c6797720308d

$ node /Users/tim/JSI/GIT/openclaw/dist/index.js health --json
ok: true
plugins.errors: []

$ ps -p 80878 -o pid=,comm=,args=
80878 /Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app/Contents/MacOS/OpenClaw

$ codesign --verify --deep --strict --verbose=4 /Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app
/Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app: valid on disk
/Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app: satisfies its Designated Requirement

$ codesign -dv --verbose=4 /Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app
Identifier=ai.openclaw.mac
Authority=Developer ID Application: Peter Steinberger (Y5PE65HELJ)
Authority=Developer ID Certification Authority

$ spctl --assess --type execute -vv /Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app

$ xattr -l /Users/tim/Applications/OpenClaw.app
com.apple.macl:

```

- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live terminal output above from the real local OpenClaw setup, including OpenClaw Gateway health output, the running GUI process path, strict code-signature verification output, signing/notarization metadata, Gatekeeper acceptance output, and extended attributes showing no quarantine attribute.

- **Observed result after fix:** The live OpenClaw Gateway reported healthy with no plugin errors, loopback RPC reported `ok: true` with `admin_capable`, the running GUI app resolved to the expected OpenClaw executable, strict signature verification passed, the app satisfied its designated requirement, and Gatekeeper accepted the app as a Notarized Developer ID application. The reduced PR code path remains provider-neutral and no longer touches generic `chat.send` request/schema or chat final/error delivery.

Full media proof for mic streaming, assistant audio playback, cancel/barge-in, omitted-transport fallback, and clean visible transcript text still needs to be captured by a maintainer or CI environment that can sign and launch a PR-head macOS app. Android and HTTP HUD mirroring are intentionally out of scope for this reduced PR.

target.title: perf(qqbot): narrow tool discovery cold load
target.body:
## Profile Evidence

| Run | CPU profile | Submit -> first model request | Request -> visible response | QQBot tool-discovery/register | First-turn embedded duration |
| --- | --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| Before | `.artifacts/tui-local-cpu/full-surface-1780700761191/openclaw-tui-99042-2026-06-05T23-06-02-328Z.cpuprofile` | 12,683ms | 17ms | 4,640.8ms | 10,824ms |
| After | `.artifacts/tui-local-cpu/full-surface-narrow-tools-1780700900305/openclaw-tui-17302-2026-06-05T23-08-29-149Z.cpuprofile` | 10,387ms | 23ms | 1,268.9ms | 6,563ms |

CPU profile summaries showed the first turn is cold module loading, not provider latency: top samples were `dist/jiti.cjs`, Babel transforms, `package_json_reader`, `fs` stat/open/read, and ESM compile. The mocked local Responses server returned in ~20ms; the delay happened before the request was sent.

## Summary

- Route QQBot `tool-discovery` registration through the narrow tool registration module instead of the broad public `api.ts` barrel.
- Keep full QQBot registration on the channel-entry path for normal runtime registration.
- Lazy-load the QQ sender auth helper only when `qqbot_channel_api` executes, so tool registration does not import messaging sender/runtime code.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/commands/framework-registration.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/tools/remind.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/tools/remind-logic.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-entry-contract.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check`
- Profiled `pnpm openclaw tui --local` with an isolated config and mocked local Responses endpoint before and after the patch.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 ✓ 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix — Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence — Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved — image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending — covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511

Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 ✓ 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix — Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence — Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved — image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending — covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511

target.title: fix(models): mask paste-token input in CLI auth prompt
target.body:
## Summary What problem does this PR solve? `openclaw models auth paste-token --provider

` echoes the pasted bearer/setup token in cleartext at the TTY prompt, exposing the secret to anyone who can see the screen. Why does this matter now? This is the third and last of three audited credential-prompt TTY echo leak sites. The first two were fixed in PR #76693 (onboarding) and PR #90571 (gateway password / Repro B). This closes Repro C. What is the intended outcome? The `paste-token` prompt renders as masked bullets, matching the already-masked sibling command `paste-api-key`. What is intentionally out of scope? No changes to stored values — masking is render-only; the persisted auth profile value remains the real typed string. What does success look like? Running `openclaw models auth paste-token --provider anthropic` shows a masked (bullet) prompt instead of cleartext echo. What should reviewers focus on? The one-line change in `modelsAuthPasteTokenCommand` (`masked: false → true`). Test reroutes from `clackText` to `clackPassword` mock. The new regression test that locks the masked routing. ## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes # (none — discovered during credential-prompt audit) Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? Related #76693 (onboarding password mask, merged) Related #90571 (Repro B — gateway password mask, open) Related #91059 (Repro A — gateway token mask, open) Related #90899 ([verify] macOS TTY rendering) Related #90900 ([verify] Linux TTY rendering) Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No — discovered and fixed as part of a self-initiated credential-prompt audit. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: `paste-token` echoes typed token in cleartext at the TTY prompt - Real environment tested: Windows 10 (build 19045.7291), native PowerShell window - Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node openclaw.mjs models auth paste-token --provider anthropic` — prompt renders bullets - Evidence after fix: - Observed result after fix: Token input renders as masked bullets instead of cleartext - What was not tested: macOS and Linux TTY rendering (see #90899, #90900) - Proof limitations or environment constraints: Windows-only repro environment. `@clack/prompts` `password()` is cross-platform; verify issues track macOS/Linux rendering separately. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? ``` pnpm build ; pnpm check ; pnpm test ``` `pnpm check` failed on pre-existing TypeScript errors in `extensions/discord/src/` (unrelated to this diff — confirmed via `git diff origin/main --name-only` showing only `src/commands/models/auth.ts` and `src/commands/models/auth.test.ts`). What regression coverage was added or updated? Added `"reads the pasted token through the masked password prompt"` test that asserts `clackPassword` is called and `clackText` is not called. Rerouted 9 existing paste-token tests from `clackText` mock to `clackPassword` mock. What failed before this fix, if known? After flipping `masked: true`, all 9 paste-token tests that primed `clackText` failed. Fixed by rerouting each to `clackPassword`. If no test was added, why not? A regression test was added. ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes — the `paste-token` TTY prompt now masks input as bullets. The stored auth profile value is unchanged (masking is render-only; `normalizeSecretInput` runs in both paths). Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes — improved: token is no longer visible in the terminal during entry. No behavioral change to auth logic. What is the highest-risk area? `readPastedSecret` dispatches to `password()` vs `text()` purely off the `masked` boolean. No other call site or stored value is affected. How is that risk mitigated? The regression test locks the masked routing. The sibling `paste-api-key` already uses `masked: true` as the reference implementation. ## Current review state What is the next action? Confirm CI is green, then flip to Ready for Review. What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? - macOS TTY rendering verification — #90899 - Linux TTY rendering verification — #90900 Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? None yet (draft PR). --- **AI-assisted PR:** - [x] Marked as AI-assisted in the description - [x] Human-run real behavior proof from your own setup (before/after screenshots attached above) - [ ] Prompts or session logs (session logs available on request) - [x] Confirmed understanding of the code change - [x] Will resolve bot review conversations after addressing them

target.title: fix(qwen): enable qwen3.6-plus on Coding Plan CN, correct reasoning flag
target.body:
## Summary

- **What changed**: Enables `qwen3.6-plus` on the DashScope Coding Plan CN endpoint (`coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) and corrects its `reasoning` flag (`false` → `true`).
  - `extensions/qwen/models.ts`: `reasoning: false` → `reasoning: true` on the `qwen3.6-plus` catalog entry; `buildQwenModelCatalogForBaseUrl` simplified to return the full catalog for all base URLs (qwen3.6-plus no longer excluded from Coding Plan URLs).
  - `extensions/qwen/openclaw.plugin.json`: removes the two `modelCatalog.suppressions` entries that blocked `qwen3.6-plus` from Coding Plan configurations — confirmed available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`.
  - `extensions/qwen/index.ts`: removes `normalizeConfig` (which stripped `qwen3.6-plus` from Coding Plan `providerConfig.models`); no longer needed after manifest suppression removal.
- **Why it matters**: `qwen3.6-plus` is available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com` (DashScope CN Coding Plan). The existing suppression incorrectly hid it from Coding Plan users. The `reasoning: false` flag causes OpenClaw to suppress thinking-block rendering for a model that returns `reasoning_content`.
- **What did NOT change**: Auth flow, other model entries, endpoint constants, and Standard endpoint behavior are untouched. Global Coding Plan (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) behavior is not claimed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations (Qwen / DashScope provider catalog)
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Standalone catalog update; no linked issue.
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: `qwen3.6-plus` was suppressed on Coding Plan CN and showed `reasoning: false`. After this patch it is available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com` and the reasoning flag is accurate.
- **Environment tested**: DashScope CN Coding Plan endpoint (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`), live API key, direct HTTP.
- **Steps run after the patch**:
  1. Sent a completion request for `qwen3.6-plus` to `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1` with a live Coding Plan API key.
  2. Inspected response for `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` confirming thinking-mode support.
- **Observed result**: `qwen3.6-plus` accepted on Coding Plan CN endpoint; `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` present in response, confirming `reasoning: true` is correct.
- **Evidence**:

<details>
<summary>Live response — qwen3.6-plus on coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com</summary>

```text
POST https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1/chat/completions
Authorization: Bearer sk-<redacted>
```

```json
{
  "model": "qwen3.6-plus",
  "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What is 2+2? Answer in one word."}],
  "max_tokens": 50
}
```

```json
{
  "choices": [{
    "message": {
      "content": "Four",
      "reasoning_content": "Here's a thinking process:\n\n1. Question: \"What is 2+2?\"\n2. 2 + 2 = 4\n3. One word: Four",
      "role": "assistant"
    },
    "finish_reason": "stop",
    "index": 0
  }],
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 22,
    "completion_tokens": 227,
    "total_tokens": 249,
    "completion_tokens_details": {"reasoning_tokens": 221, "text_tokens": 227}
  },
  "model": "qwen3.6-plus"
}
```

`qwen3.6-plus` accepted on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`; `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` confirmed.

</details>

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- Risk summary: Catalog metadata change only. Removes an incorrect suppression; no new endpoint, auth flow, or runtime surface introduced.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Endpoint: DashScope CN Coding Plan (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`)
- Auth: DashScope Coding Plan API key (`sk-<redacted>`)

### Steps

1. POST completion request with `"model": "qwen3.6-plus"` to `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`.
2. Inspect response for `reasoning_content` and `completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens`.

### Expected

`qwen3.6-plus` responds on Coding Plan CN with `reasoning_content` present.

### Actual

Matches expected.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: `qwen3.6-plus` live on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`; `reasoning_content` present (flag corrected to `true`).
- **Edge cases checked**: None beyond CN Coding Plan endpoint.
- **What I did NOT verify**: Global Coding Plan endpoint (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) — not maintained by this contributor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — removes a suppression; Coding Plan CN users who previously could not select `qwen3.6-plus` can now do so. No existing working configuration is broken.
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: `qwen3.6-plus` may not be available on all Coding Plan CN accounts (account-level activation required).
  - Mitigation: Users without access will receive a vendor-side model-not-found error, same as any unavailable model; no crash or data loss.
- **Risk**: Global Coding Plan (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) behavior unknown — suppression removed for that endpoint too as a side effect.
  - Mitigation: If Global Coding Plan does not support `qwen3.6-plus`, affected users will see a vendor error. A follow-up can re-add a suppression scoped to `coding-intl` only if confirmed unavailable there.

target.title: fix(exec-approvals): resolve path/socket from the state dir (honor OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR)
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `exec-approvals.{json,sock}` were resolved from the home default instead of the canonical OpenClaw state dir.
- Relocated installs using `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` could split approval state or fail exec calls when `$HOME/.openclaw` was symlinked.
- Changed files: `src/infra/exec-approvals.ts`, `src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts`, `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts`, `src/config/paths.ts`, `src/infra/state-dir.ts`.

Why does this matter now?

- `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` is documented and supported, but exec approvals were not honoring that state-dir override.

What is the intended outcome?

- Exec approvals resolve through `resolveStateDir()`.
- Default installs keep the same `<home>/.openclaw/exec-approvals.*` paths.
- Relocated installs use `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` for approval storage.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Approval policy, command analysis, socket protocol behavior, and config-supplied `socket.path` overrides.

What does success look like?

- Default installs keep existing behavior.
- Relocated installs keep approval files and sockets under the configured state dir.
- Symlinked state-dir components are still refused by the exec-approval symlink guard.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The state-dir ownership boundary in `src/infra/exec-approvals.ts`.
- The trusted-root selection for the symlink guard.
- The default-path and relocated-path regression coverage.

<details>
<summary>Summary guidance</summary>

This PR description is the contributor's durable explanation of the change. Write it for human maintainers first; ClawSweeper and Barnacle use the same text to understand intent, proof, risk, and current review state.

Describe the intent and outcome in 2-5 bullets. Avoid restating the diff; reviewers and bots can read the changed files.

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

</details>

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90971

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

- Related state-dir consumers: `src/infra/device-auth-store.ts`, `src/infra/restart-sentinel.ts`, `src/infra/push-web.ts`.
- Related docs: https://docs.openclaw.ai/help/environment/

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Yes, owner request.

<details>
<summary>Linked context guidance</summary>

Link the issue, PR, discussion, maintainer request, or owner request that explains why this PR should exist. Maintainer context helps reviewers and automation distinguish intended work from drive-by churn.

</details>

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: exec-approvals path resolution under `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, including symlinked-home relocated installs.
- Real environment tested: macOS local checkout, Node 24.16.0.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx scripts/check-import-cycles.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts src/config/paths.test.ts`; `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/exec-approvals.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts src/config/paths.ts src/infra/state-dir.ts`; `git diff --check --cached`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): `Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).`; `[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 11.85s`; `All matched files use the correct format.`
- Observed result after fix: approvals resolve to the configured state dir, default installs keep the same paths, and the symlink guard still refuses a symlinked state-dir component.
- What was not tested: full multi-host gateway/live approval run.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: local macOS proof only; GitHub CI will re-run on the pushed PR head. The broad Madge import-cycle check still reports an existing upstream-main SCC and is not claimed as passing here.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): relocated installs previously wrote approvals to `$HOME/.openclaw`, and symlinked-home setups could fail exec calls with `Refusing to traverse symlink in exec approvals path`.

<details>
<summary>Real behavior proof guidance</summary>

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only.

Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes. Terminal screenshots, copied live output, redacted runtime logs, recordings, and linked artifacts count.

If your environment cannot produce the ideal proof, explain that under `Proof limitations or environment constraints` so reviewers and ClawSweeper can direct the next step properly.

Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

</details>

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node --import tsx scripts/check-import-cycles.ts` -> `0 runtime value cycle(s)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts src/config/paths.test.ts` -> 3 shards, 3 files, 42 tests passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/exec-approvals.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts src/config/paths.ts src/infra/state-dir.ts` -> clean.
- `git diff --check --cached` -> clean.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added `src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts` coverage for HOME-only defaults, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` precedence, and relocated symlink refusal.
- Updated `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts` expectations for state-dir-aware approval paths.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Relocated installs used `$HOME/.openclaw` instead of `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.
- Symlinked-home relocated installs could fail exec calls with the symlink guard refusal.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added and updated for the touched behavior.

<details>
<summary>Testing guidance</summary>

List focused commands, not every incidental check. CI is useful support, but external PRs still need real behavior proof above when behavior changes.

</details>

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- Yes. Relocated-state installs now read and write exec approvals in `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`; default installs keep the same paths.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- Yes. `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` now controls exec-approval storage like the other state-owned files. No new config key or migration was added.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- Yes. This touches command-execution approval storage only; approval policy and socket protocol are unchanged, and symlink refusal is preserved.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Trusted-root selection for the approval path symlink guard.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Default installs keep a trusted root equivalent to the previous home root.
- Relocated installs are covered by state-dir precedence and symlink-refusal tests.

<details>
<summary>Risk guidance</summary>

Use this for author judgment that is not obvious from the diff. ClawSweeper can see touched files, but it cannot know which behavior you think is risky, why the risk is acceptable, or what mitigation reviewers should verify.

</details>

target.title: feat(slack): add ignoreOtherMentions channel config
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `ignoreOtherMentions` option for Slack channel config, mirroring the existing Discord guild/channel feature
- When enabled, messages that mention another user (`<@U...>`) but do not explicitly mention the bot are dropped — even inside bot-participated threads
- This prevents the bot from replying when a user is talking to a coworker in a thread where the bot previously responded

## Slack vs Discord difference

Discord's `ignoreOtherMentions` checks `!wasMentioned && !implicitMention`. The Slack version intentionally omits the `!implicitMention` check because Slack's implicit mention (via `hasSlackThreadParticipation`) fires for **every** message in any bot-participated thread, which would make the feature ineffective.

## Config example

```json5
{
  channels: {
    slack: {
      channels: {
        "*": { ignoreOtherMentions: true },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

## Changed files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/types.slack.ts` | Add `ignoreOtherMentions` to `SlackChannelConfig` |
| `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` | Add zod validation for the new field |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/channel-config.ts` | Wire through type + resolver |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts` | Drop logic before mention gating |

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm build` passes
- [x] `pnpm test -- extensions/slack/src/monitor` — 236 tests pass
- [x] Manual test on live Slack workspace: bot no longer replies when tagging a coworker in a bot thread
- [x] Bot still replies when explicitly @mentioned in the same thread

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: feat(doctor): detect local rebuild vs pristine npm release
target.body:
## Problem

A stock `openclaw update` silently overwrites locally patched installs.
Regressions return quietly because operators have no signal that the
running package has diverged from the pristine npm release.

Concrete trigger: the 2026-04-20 dist-overlay audit of
`/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw` on a production host showed
v2026.4.15 with `dist/build-info.json.builtAt = 2026-04-19` while the
npm registry reported that version was published on `2026-04-16`.
That ~3-day skew is a locally rebuilt install — a stock `update` had
wiped a sticky-fallback patch that had been applied by hand.

## Solution

Non-blocking `openclaw doctor` note that compares local build metadata
against the npm registry publish time and warns if the installed
package is a local rebuild, an unreleased version, or has corrupt
metadata.

* **Warn only.** Never fails doctor.
* **Silent on pristine or inconclusive** (no network, no cache, etc.).
* **Runs in the install-posture section** of `doctor`, right after
  `noteSourceInstallIssues`.

## Signals

* Reads `<packageRoot>/dist/build-info.json` → `{version, commit, builtAt}`
  and `<packageRoot>/package.json.version`.
* Fetches `https://registry.npmjs.org/openclaw`, inspects
  `.time[<version>]`.
* **rebuild** — `builtAt - publishedAt >= 60 min`.
* **unreleased** — version not present in registry `.time`.
* **corrupt** — local `build-info.json` / `package.json` missing or
  malformed.
* **inconclusive** — registry fetch failed and no usable cache
  (silent, to avoid false positives on air-gapped installs).
* **pristine** — otherwise.

Registry response cached at `~/.openclaw/rebuild-guard-cache.json`
with a 6h TTL. Honors `OPENCLAW_NPM_REGISTRY_FILE` for tests and an
`offline: true` option for environments that should never hit the
network.

## Test coverage

`src/commands/doctor-rebuild-guard.test.ts` (8/8 green):

* pristine within the skew window
* rebuild when `builtAt` is >= 60 min after npm publish
* unreleased (version not in registry)
* corrupt (missing / malformed `build-info.json`)
* inconclusive (fetch throws, no cache) → silent
* offline mode (no network call, decides from local only)
* cache hit (second call, within TTL, does not call `fetch`)
* `noteRebuildGuardHealth` emission matrix: emits for
  `rebuild` / `unreleased` / `corrupt`; silent for
  `pristine` / `inconclusive`

## Gates

* `pnpm lint` — pass
* `pnpm tsgo:all` (typecheck) — pass
* `pnpm build` — pass
* `pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/doctor-rebuild-guard.test.ts` — 8/8

## Sample doctor output (rebuilt host)

```
└ Install ────────────────────────────────────────
  Local OpenClaw install differs from the pristine npm release.
  - Install: /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw
  - Version: 2026.4.15
  - Commit: 8abd1fc
  - builtAt: 2026-04-19T08:42:11Z
  - npm publish: 2026-04-16T19:03:47Z
  - Skew: 4197 minutes ahead of npm
  - Reason: rebuild-after-publish
  Confirm every local patch has a matching upstream PR before running `openclaw update`.
```

## Related

A companion **Layer-1 bash guard** already ships in operator
workspaces (installed as a systemd `ExecStartPre`) to surface the same
warning on daemon start. This PR makes the same check native to every
`openclaw` install so end users benefit from it too, not just
operators who maintain local workspaces.

commands.author: yu-xin-c

target.title: fix(agents): guard CLI MCP resume reuse
target.body:
### Summary
- Invalidate stored CLI session bindings when they have a strict `mcpResumeHash` but the current run cannot provide one.
- Add regression coverage for the one-sided resume-hash case while keeping legacy `mcpConfigHash` fallback for bindings that never stored a resume hash.

### Root cause
`resolveCliSessionReuse` only compared `mcpResumeHash` when both the stored binding and current run had one. If a stored CLI session had a strict resume hash but a later run could not compute the current resume hash, the helper fell back to the broader `mcpConfigHash` comparison and could reuse a session without proving the resumed MCP topology still matched.

### Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: CLI sessions created with a strict bundled-MCP resume hash should not be reused by a later run that cannot prove the same resumed MCP tool topology. The patch prevents a stored `mcpResumeHash` from silently downgrading to the less precise raw MCP config hash when the current run lacks `mcpResumeHash`.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/stellarfish/.codex/worktrees/5db8/openclaw-next-agent`, branch `codex/agent-runtime-followup`, macOS shell, Node `v22.22.2`, corepack pnpm `11.2.2`. The local proof commit `b391908e` has tree `119772a5c6c3fae479f0f730ed27bb98799bb50d`, matching this PR head `3535cfc0cdda80f9af679404b350954b3b961729` tree `119772a5c6c3fae479f0f730ed27bb98799bb50d`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: I ran the copied live terminal commands below after applying this patch. The first command directly invokes the changed helper and prints the observed runtime decision; the remaining commands cover regression tests, adjacent bundled-MCP resume hash behavior, and formatting.

```text
node --import tsx --input-type=module --eval '<imports resolveCliSessionReuse and calls it with stored mcpResumeHash but no current mcpResumeHash>'
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-session.test.ts --reporter verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.resume.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.test.ts --reporter verbose
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/cli-session.ts src/agents/cli-session.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal transcript / live output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
$ git rev-parse --short HEAD
b391908e
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module --eval <resolveCliSessionReuse live proof>
{
  "observed": {
    "invalidatedReason": "mcp"
  }
}

$ node --version
v22.22.2
$ corepack pnpm --version
11.2.2
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-session.test.ts --reporter verbose
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/stellarfish/.codex/worktrees/5db8/openclaw-next-agent
✓ |unit-fast| ../../src/agents/cli-session.test.ts > cli-session helpers > invalidates resume-hash bindings when the current run cannot prove the resumed MCP topology 0ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  17 passed (17)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.resume.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.test.ts --reporter verbose
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/stellarfish/.codex/worktrees/5db8/openclaw-next-agent
✓ |unit-fast| ../../src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.resume.test.ts > prepareCliBundleMcpConfig resume hash > stabilizes the resume hash when only the OpenClaw loopback port changes 136ms
✓ |unit-fast| ../../src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.resume.test.ts > prepareCliBundleMcpConfig resume hash > changes the resume hash when stable MCP semantics change 18ms

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  14 passed (14)

$ ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/cli-session.ts src/agents/cli-session.test.ts
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 9ms on 2 files using 1 threads.
```

Observed result after fix: The direct helper invocation returns `{ "invalidatedReason": "mcp" }` when a stored binding has `mcpResumeHash` but the current run lacks one. Existing bundled-MCP resume hash behavior still passes: port-only loopback changes keep the same resume hash, semantic MCP changes produce a different resume hash, and formatting remains clean.

What was not tested: I did not run a real Claude CLI network turn. This patch is limited to OpenClaw's CLI session reuse decision helper and its bundled-MCP resume hash preparation tests.

### Verification
- `node --import tsx --input-type=module --eval '<imports resolveCliSessionReuse and calls it with stored mcpResumeHash but no current mcpResumeHash>'`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-session.test.ts --reporter verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.resume.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.test.ts --reporter verbose`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/cli-session.ts src/agents/cli-session.test.ts`

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: [AI-assisted] Persist agent-run error replies in chat history
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: A started Control UI agent run could surface a terminal provider/runtime error live, but `chat.history` after refresh did not retain an assistant error turn for that failed run.
- Solution: Treat agent-run failure replies as real error payloads and make Gateway persist exactly one display-safe assistant error transcript entry for returned agent errors, even when the runtime emitted only an internal error stub.
- What changed: Runner failure payloads are marked `isError`, Gateway appends a deduped `<runId>:assistant-error` transcript entry for returned agent errors, and regression coverage now locks the returned-error, runtime-stub, and duplicate/idempotency paths.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No provider behavior changes, no delivery routing changes, no config/lifecycle changes, no raw transcript writes, and no dependency or lockfile changes.

## Motivation

- A user should not lose the visible assistant error after refreshing the Control UI. If a started run fails before normal assistant output, the live error event and durable history should agree.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #89197
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A real Gateway/WebSocket Control UI run that fails at provider transport now leaves exactly one assistant error in refreshed `chat.history`.
- Real environment tested: Disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout with the patched PR branch, real Gateway server startup, real WebSocket RPC client, channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and an intentionally unreachable OpenAI-compatible base URL to trigger the provider failure without secrets.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -- -t 'assistant error|returned agent-run error payloads|runtime emitted an internal error stub'
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.error-broadcast.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- -t 'Agent failed before reply|assistant error|fallback candidate|error stub'
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node --import tsx ../real-chat-history-proof.ts after-gateway-force-v3
/usr/bin/time -v node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Real runtime proof:
- provider transport failed with fetch failed / Connection error
- agent.wait status: error
- immediateHistoryCount: 3
- refreshHistoryCount: 3
- immediateAssistantCount: 1
- refreshAssistantCount: 1
- chatErrorEventCount: 1
- result: ONE_ASSISTANT_ERROR_IN_REFRESHED_HISTORY

Focused Gateway proof: 3 passed, 102 skipped.
Adjacent Gateway proof: 107 passed.
Session tool result guard proof: 35 passed.
Runner/Followup focused proof: 8 passed, 214 skipped.
git diff --check: clean.
```

- Observed result after fix: The live provider failure still broadcasts the expected chat error, and both immediate and post-reconnect `chat.history` contain one assistant error text: `Agent failed before reply: LLM request failed: network connection error.`
- What was not tested: Full repository-wide test suite, successful `tsgo` core on this testserver, real provider credentials, and a browser screenshot.
- Before evidence:

```text
Unpatched main proof for the same real Gateway/WebSocket/provider-failure path:
- sendStatus: started
- agent.wait status: ok
- immediateHistoryCount: 2
- refreshHistoryCount: 2
- immediateAssistantCount: 0
- refreshAssistantCount: 0
- chatErrorEventCount: 0
- result: ASSISTANT_ERROR_HISTORY_MISMATCH
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Runner final failure replies were not consistently marked as error payloads, and Gateway treated the runtime assistant-error callback as if it guaranteed a Control UI-visible `chat.history` assistant turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Previous coverage asserted live error broadcast behavior but did not prove refreshed `chat.history` retained an assistant error after a started real runtime failure.
- Contributing context (if known): Runtime/session guard error stubs can be internal transcript-only markers; Gateway still needs to durably mirror returned terminal agent errors into the Control UI history path.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`, and the temporary real Gateway/WebSocket proof harness.
- Scenario the test should lock in: A started run returns an agent error; Gateway appends one assistant error transcript entry, keeps the live error terminal event, survives reconnect/history refresh, and does not duplicate the assistant error.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The source tests cover the exact Gateway and runner seams, while the real proof exercises server startup, WebSocket RPC, provider transport failure, `agent.wait`, and refreshed `chat.history` in one flow.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing broadcast tests covered visible error delivery but not durable assistant transcript history.
- If no new test is added, why not: New regression assertions were added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Control UI users should now see the same failed assistant turn after refresh that they saw live when a started agent run fails before normal assistant output.

## Diagram

```text
real provider/runtime failure
  -> runner returns final failure payload marked isError
  -> Gateway returnedAgentErrorPayloads branch
  -> append one sanitized assistant error transcript entry with <runId>:assistant-error
  -> broadcast existing chat error terminal event
  -> reconnect chat.history still includes one assistant error
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? No new scope; the change writes a sanitized assistant error to the same session transcript path already used for Gateway assistant persistence.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Not applicable.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux disposable test checkout
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw repository scripts with installed dependencies
- Model/provider: OpenAI-compatible provider configured to an unreachable local base URL for deterministic transport failure; no real credentials used
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI-style Gateway WebSocket RPC; external channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`
- Relevant config (redacted): Dummy redacted API key, local unreachable base URL, temporary state directory

### Steps

1. Start the real Gateway server from the patched checkout.
2. Connect a WebSocket client and send `chat.send` for a Control UI session.
3. Trigger a deterministic provider transport failure through the unreachable model base URL.
4. Wait for the run with `agent.wait`.
5. Read `chat.history` immediately.
6. Reconnect a fresh WebSocket client and read `chat.history` again.
7. Count assistant text messages and chat error events.

### Expected

- The run surfaces a live terminal error.
- Refreshed `chat.history` contains exactly one assistant error message for the failed run.
- The assistant error is not duplicated.

### Actual

- Before: refreshed `chat.history` had zero assistant messages for the failed run.
- After: refreshed `chat.history` had exactly one assistant error message and one chat error event.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
Before real proof result: ASSISTANT_ERROR_HISTORY_MISMATCH
After real proof result: ONE_ASSISTANT_ERROR_IN_REFRESHED_HISTORY
After source proof: Gateway focused 3/3, Gateway adjacent 107/107, session guard 35/35, Runner/Followup focused 8/8.
TypeScript core check on the testserver did not produce diagnostics; the process was terminated by signal 9 at about 1.78 GB RSS, so this is reported as a testserver resource blocker rather than a green typecheck claim.
```

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Returned agent-run error payload detection, runner final failure `isError` marking, Gateway assistant-error transcript append, runtime internal error-stub callback interaction, idempotency key behavior, real provider transport failure, immediate history, refreshed history after reconnect, and existing chat error event behavior.
- Edge cases checked: Runtime internal error stub does not suppress the Control UI-visible returned error transcript; Gateway writes one `<runId>:assistant-error` entry for the returned terminal error; no dependency or lockfile files changed.
- What you did **not** verify: Full repo-wide suite, successful `tsgo` core on the resource-limited testserver, real external provider credentials, screenshot/recording, and production channels.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Not applicable.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A runtime-internal error stub could suppress the Control UI-visible returned error transcript.
  - Mitigation: Returned terminal agent errors now force the Gateway assistant-error transcript append while retaining the existing idempotency key.
- Risk: Gateway could duplicate assistant errors.
  - Mitigation: The transcript append uses a stable `<runId>:assistant-error` idempotency key and the source tests cover the one-message behavior.
- Risk: Error text could bypass display sanitization.
  - Mitigation: The Gateway fallback still uses `sanitizeAssistantDisplayText` before transcript append.
- Risk: Dependency Guard could require security approval.
  - Mitigation: No dependency manifests, lockfiles, or dependency blocks were changed.
